test: PPP half-life / real-exchange-rate mean-reversion (frozen pre-registered protocol)
data: FRED DEX* FX 1971-2026 (55yr; KRW/HKD 45yr) + OECD CPI; US CPIAUCSL
robust_finding: Floating real exchange rates behave like near-random-walks (ADF cannot reject unit root, p>0.25 for ALL floaters) — the classic Rogoff PPP puzzle. This half is solid and expected.
suggestive_finding: The one USD-anchored currency (HKD) has a far more stable, stationary measured rate (ADF p=0.000, half-life 1.4yr, variance 0.028) than any floater.
falsification: Protocol G1/G2 NOT triggered — anchored reverts faster + floaters can't reject unit root. Claim SURVIVES on this test.
CRITICAL_CAVEAT: HKD result is PROVISIONAL and partly TAUTOLOGICAL: no Hong Kong CPI was available, so HKD's q used the NOMINAL rate only. A nominal peg fixes S by construction, so its 'stability' is largely the peg mechanism, not evidence about the REAL rate. A valid HKD real-rate test REQUIRES Hong Kong CPI (P_HK). Until then, HKD's stationarity is not a clean real-exchange-rate result.
what_it_shows: Floaters exhibit the PPP puzzle (robust). A hard nominal anchor yields a more stable measured rate (suggestive, but needs HK CPI to be non-tautological).
what_it_does_NOT_show: That a CS-style INFLATION anchor (nominal FLOAT + rule-fixed inflation) would stabilize the REAL rate. HKD is a hard nominal peg, not that object. The CS case remains BRACKETED between HKD (hard peg) and floaters, unobserved.
half_life_precision_warning: Point half-lives are notoriously imprecise (literature CIs span years). Only the ADF stationary/non-stationary distinction is reliable; do not cite JPY '24.6yr' etc. as precise.
verdict: Stronger and cleaner than the monthly Line A, and survives its own falsification — BUT the anchored side rests on HKD, whose result needs Hong Kong CPI to not be a nominal-peg tautology. NET: promising, provisional, still does NOT upgrade the audio. The honest statement is 'floating real rates don't mean-revert (puzzle); a hard anchor appears to help; CS-style inflation-anchoring untested and bracketed.'
next_to_make_it_solid: Hong Kong CPI (make HKD a real-rate test not nominal)
the CS object itself is unobservable — state the bracket honestly rather than claiming the anchored point
